title: teucrium ronnigeri subsp. lagunae
intro: Teucrium ronnigeri subsp. lagunae, also known as Lagune Germander, is a subspecies of the Germander plant, a type of herbaceous perennial plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety and stress, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Teucrium ronnigeri subsp. lagunae is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for easy consumption and utilization of its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", teucrium ronnigeri subsp. lagunae, which is a part of the genus Teucrium used in Kurdish traditional medicine, may be a natural source of specialized metabolites that are potentially beneficial to human health, particularly for treating jaundice, stomachache, and abdominal problems.
